common.heroFooter: Free assessment • No obligation • Your information is private

home.heroTitle: Injured in an Accident? See What Your Case May Be Worth in 60 Seconds
home.heroSubtitle: Answer a few questions about your accident and see:
home.heroItem1: If you may have a case
home.heroItem2: What your case may be worth
home.heroItem3: How long it could take to settle
home.trustBar: Helping California injury victims understand their options
home.trust1: Thousands of injury case patterns analyzed
home.trust2: Optional matching with vetted personal injury attorneys
home.trust4: Plain-English case value and next-step guidance
home.howItWorksIntro: Start with the facts you know now. ClearCaseIQ turns your answers into a plain-English case snapshot and next-step options.
home.step1Desc: Answer guided questions about the accident, injuries, treatment, and insurance.
home.step2Desc: See case strength, possible value range, timeline, and missing evidence.
home.step3Desc: For some straightforward claims, you may be able to prepare and send a settlement demand yourself. You can also request attorney review before signing any release.
home.commonCaseTypes: Injury Cases We Can Help Screen
home.commonCaseTypesDesc: Start with any accident type. If your case needs a lawyer, we can help route it to the right kind of attorney.
home.reportIncludes: What You Get After the Free Assessment
home.reportIncludesDesc: A practical case snapshot you can use before deciding whether to handle next steps yourself or request attorney review.
home.reportItem5: Self-help demand package for straightforward claims
home.whyUse: Why Start Here Before Calling a Lawyer?
home.whyUseDesc: Get clarity, control, and next steps before you share your case broadly.
home.whyUseItem1: Know whether your case may be worth pursuing before you spend days calling firms.
home.whyUseItem2: Understand the value drivers insurers and attorneys usually look at.
home.whyUseItem3: See what documents could strengthen your claim.
home.whyUseItem4: Choose the path that fits: self-help demand package, attorney review, or save your report for later.
home.step3DescAlt: For some straightforward claims, you may be able to prepare and send a settlement demand yourself. For serious injuries, disputed liability, liens, or any release, attorney review is strongly recommended.
home.testimonial1Quote: I finally understood what my case could be worth before I ever spoke to a lawyer. The process felt respectful and clear.
home.testimonial2Quote: Clear steps, plain language, and I knew my data was private. That mattered after the accident.
home.testimonial3Quote: Within a day I was connected to a firm that actually handles injury cases like mine—not a random call center.
home.finalCtaHelper: Get your case summary, estimate, and next-step checklist.
home.heroReassurance: Takes about 60 seconds · No signup required · 100% confidential
home.securityBadge: Encrypted intake · HIPAA-aware handling of medical details
home.ratingLabel: Rated 5 out of 5 by example users
home.legalDisclaimer: ClearCaseIQ is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice, and no attorney-client relationship is created by using this site. Case estimates are informational only and are not a guarantee of any outcome. Attorney review is optional and occurs only with your consent.
home.faqQ1: Is the case assessment really free?
home.faqA1: Yes. The case assessment is free, requires no signup to start, and there is no obligation to hire an attorney.
home.faqQ2: Will my information be shared with attorneys?
home.faqA2: Only with your consent. Your intake is private and encrypted, and we share your details with an attorney only if you choose to request attorney review or a match.
home.faqQ3: How accurate is the estimated case value?
home.faqA3: The estimate is an informational range based on patterns in similar injury cases and the facts you provide. It is not legal advice or a guarantee of any specific result.
home.faqA4: Most people complete the guided questions in about 60 seconds and receive a case snapshot immediately.

footer.tagline: Free injury case assessments and attorney matching.
footer.privacyLine: Private intake. No obligation. Attorney sharing only with your consent.
footer.trustRow: Private • Secure • No Obligation • Attorney Sharing Only With Consent
footer.disclaimer: ClearCaseIQ is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. Use of this platform does not create an attorney-client relationship. Attorney review is optional and only occurs with user consent.

intake.timePromise: Free case assessment • About 60 seconds • No obligation
intake.startHeadline: Find out if you may have a personal injury case in 60 seconds
intake.startHelper: Answer a few guided questions. You will get a case snapshot, evidence checklist, and next-step options.
intake.report_contactTitle: Where should we send your report?
intake.report_contactDesc: Optional — add an email or phone so you can access your report later and we can follow up if you request attorney review.
intake.report_contactTrust: Private. We only share your case with attorneys if you choose attorney review.
intake.skipReassurance: Most people finish in about 60 seconds. You can skip anything you do not know.
intake.answerReassurance: Answer what you know. You can skip uncertain details now and update your case after the report is created.
intake.privacyNote: Private intake. We only share your case with attorneys if you choose attorney review.
intake.tapToContinue: Tap any option to continue automatically

intake.review_whatHappenedHelper: Plain-language accident details help attorneys understand fault faster.
intake.review_whereWhenHelper: This supports venue and deadline checks.
intake.review_injuriesHelper: It is okay if this is only an early estimate.
intake.review_treatmentHelper: Medical treatment details are one of the strongest value signals.
intake.review_valuationHelper: Prior injuries, body parts, procedures, surgery, and future care can change settlement and trial values.
intake.review_insuranceHelper: Coverage, liens, income impact, and policy limits can improve estimate accuracy.
intake.review_legalHelper: Fault, insurance contact, representation, and settlement history shape attorney interest.
intake.review_documentsHelper: No documents yet is okay. You can still get a preliminary assessment.
intake.insight_imaging: Diagnostic imaging may improve estimate confidence.
intake.insight_injections: Injections or procedures can affect settlement posture.
intake.insight_surgery: Surgery being discussed may increase trial exposure.
intake.insight_missedWork: Missed work may increase economic damages.
intake.insight_noPrior: No prior injuries reported for the same areas.
intake.insight_prior: Prior injuries may affect causation and settlement compression.
intake.insight_offerAnchor: A prior offer gives the model a useful settlement anchor.
intake.insight_fault: Clearer fault facts can improve settlement leverage.
intake.insight_uploads: Uploaded documents improve confidence in the assessment.
intake.insight_fallback1: Your report will highlight missing details that can improve the estimate later.
intake.insight_fallback2: Treatment, documents, and fault details can refine settlement confidence.

intake.when_exactHelper: Most accurate — gives you a precise filing deadline.

intake.when_addLocationForDeadline: Add your state and county below to see your filing deadline.
intake.narrative_helper: A few sentences is enough. Don't worry about legal terms.
intake.narrative_placeholder: Example: I was stopped at a red light on Main St. when another car hit me from behind. The police came to the scene and I went to urgent care the next day.
intake.narrative_tip: Tip: what happened, fault, witnesses, photos, or report.
intake.hint_product_have: Do you still have the product?
intake.contact_saveDesc: We'll send you a link to return later and your case report when it's ready.
intake.badge_noObligation: No obligation to hire an attorney

Msg & data rates may apply. Message frequency varies. Reply STOP to opt out, HELP for help. Consent is not a condition of service. See our

intake.treatment_helper: Select everything that applies. It is okay if treatment is still being scheduled.
intake.treatment_tip: Medical records and bills usually improve estimate confidence more than any other document type.
intake.treatment_skip: I have not had treatment yet
intake.injuryDetails_helper: These answers improve the accuracy of your case value estimate.
intake.injuryTreatment_helper: Tell us about the care you've had and how you're doing now.
intake.injuryDetails_confidenceTip: Upload imaging or treatment records to improve accuracy.
intake.injuryDetails_whereInjuredHelper: Select all body regions that hurt or were diagnosed.
intake.injuryDetails_otherInjuryDescribe: Describe your injuries in your own words
intake.injuryDetails_otherInjuryPlaceholder: e.g., chest pain, broken ribs, internal injuries, anxiety or emotional distress...
intake.injuryDetails_testingHelper: Select any testing or treatment that has happened or is scheduled.
intake.injuryDetails_diagnosesQuestion: Have doctors mentioned any of these diagnoses?
intake.injuryDetails_diagnosesHelper: Select known findings like fractures, TBI, concussion, or herniation.
intake.injuryDetails_lifeImpact: How has this affected your life?
intake.injuryDetails_lifeImpactHelper: These details help estimate pain and daily disruption. Work and income impact comes later.
intake.injuryDetails_headSymptoms: Did you experience any of these?
intake.injuryDetails_additionalInfoHelper: These details can improve your case value estimate and attorney matching.
intake.injuryDetails_priorBodyAreas: Prior injuries to these body areas?
intake.injuryDetails_priorHelper: Have you previously injured the same areas involved in this accident?
intake.injuryDetails_dailyLifeQuestion: How has the injury affected your daily life?
intake.injuryDetails_dailyLifeHelper: This is how we account for pain & suffering — the ways your injury affects your quality of life beyond medical bills and missed work. Select all that apply.
intake.injuryDetails_whyMattersBody: Pain, daily life impacts, and future treatment can affect how attorneys and insurers evaluate your case.
intake.injuryDetails_treatmentReceivedHelper: Select all the care and testing you've had so far.
intake.injuryDetails_treatmentRecapTitle: Based on what you told us earlier
intake.injuryDetails_treatmentRecapEmpty: You didn't note any treatment on the previous step — you can go back to add it.
intake.injuryDetails_imagingDetailHelper: Add scans or tests not already listed above (optional).
intake.injuryDetails_currentSymptomsQuestion: What symptoms are you experiencing now?
intake.injuryDetails_biggestImpactQuestion: What's been the biggest impact on your life?
intake.injuryDetails_aiEmpty: Select your injuries above and we'll generate a summary of your case here.
intake.injuryDetails_overviewEmpty: Select where you're injured to map it here.
intake.injuryDetails_whyBullet2: Matches you with the right attorney
intake.injuryDetails_tip1: Keep all medical bills and records
intake.injuryDetails_needHelpBody: Not sure how to answer something? You can skip it and an attorney can help you fill in the details later.
intake.injuryDetails_injectionsMore: Tell us more about the injections.
intake.injuryDetails_futureTreatmentQuestion: Has a doctor recommended future treatment?
intake.vehicle_evidenceHelper: Select everything you have or know about. Police reports, tickets, witnesses, and photos can help show who was at fault.
intake.vehicle_photos: Photos of vehicles, scene, or injuries
intake.vehicle_redLight: The other driver ran a red light or stop sign
intake.vehicle_dui: DUI, alcohol, or drug use by the other driver
intake.slip_awarenessHelper: Select everything that applies. These details help show whether the property owner may have known about the hazard or had time to fix it.
intake.evidence_helper: Optional, but uploading documents strengthens your case. Add them now or after your report.
intake.evidence_sectionEvidenceHelper: Photos, videos, and official reports from the incident.
intake.evidence_sectionMedicalHelper: Bills and records that show treatment, diagnosis, and costs.
intake.evidence_sectionInsuranceHelper: Letters, denials, offers, and adjuster communications.
intake.evidence_sectionIncomeHelper: Documents that show missed work or reduced income.
intake.evidence_uploadDocsSubtitle: The more you share, the more accurate your case assessment.
intake.evidence_aiIntro: Our AI automatically reviews everything you upload to:
intake.evidence_aiBullet1: Extract your treatment history and bills
intake.evidence_readinessSubtitle: Upload key documents to improve your case evaluation and help attorneys assess your claim.
intake.evidence_topItems: Top items that would improve your score:
intake.evidence_secureTitle: Your information is safe and secure.
intake.evidence_secureBody: All files are encrypted and only shared with attorneys if you choose to connect with them.
intake.evidence_dragDropTip: Tip: drag and drop files straight onto any row — or tap the upload button.
intake.financial_heading: How has this injury affected you financially?
intake.financial_helper: These answers help estimate the economic value of your case.
intake.financial_completeNote: You have completed the core information attorneys use to evaluate case value.
intake.financial_medicalCostsHelper: Medical bills and future care often drive case value.
intake.financial_estimateNote: A rough estimate is perfectly fine here — most people don't know exact costs this early, and "Not sure" is an OK answer. We'll refine these figures from your actual bills as your case progresses.
intake.financial_exactAmountLabel: Approximate total, if you know it
intake.financial_exactAmountHelper: Optional. A specific amount values large cases more accurately than the "$50k+" range.
intake.financial_billsCompleteQuestion: Do the bills you're providing cover all your treatment so far?
intake.financial_billsCompleteHelper: If yes, we treat your documented bills as the total. If no, your estimate is kept as a floor and we'll prompt for the rest.
intake.financial_healthInsuranceQuestion: Did you have health insurance when you received treatment?
intake.financial_insurancePaidQuestion: Did health insurance pay any medical bills?
intake.financial_whoPaidHelper: This is very important to attorneys.
intake.financial_workImpactHelper: Lost income can increase economic damages.
intake.legal_heading: A few final questions about your claim
intake.legal_helper: Liability and insurance status help us understand how attorneys may evaluate your claim.
intake.legal_vehicleInsuranceHelper: Can help identify additional sources of recovery. Most people are not sure about these details.
intake.legal_autoInsuranceQuestion: Did you have auto insurance at the time?
intake.legal_umUimHelper: Uninsured/Underinsured Motorist coverage may provide additional compensation if the other driver has little or no insurance.
intake.legal_otherPartyQuestion: Do you know anything about the other party's insurance?
intake.legal_caseReviewHelper: Who was at fault shapes liability and what can be recovered.
intake.legal_partialFaultQuestion: Could someone claim you were partially at fault?
intake.legal_insuranceStatusHelper: Tell us about your insurance and legal representation.
intake.legal_insuranceNudge: Insurance details are optional — but the more you can share, the faster your attorney can evaluate your case. You can also upload your police report or insurance card below instead.
intake.legal_whyLiability: This helps attorneys understand liability and potential insurance recovery.
intake.legal_whyInsurance: This helps us protect your rights and avoid conflicts of interest.
intake.legal_insuranceDetailsSubtitle: Policy information, claim numbers, and adjuster details.
intake.legal_pipQuestion: Do you have PIP or no-fault coverage?
intake.legal_pipHelper: Personal Injury Protection (PIP) pays your medical bills regardless of who was at fault. It's required in no-fault states like FL, NY, MI, NJ, and PA. "Not sure" is fine.
intake.legal_whatPip: PIP / no-fault coverage pays your medical bills and some lost wages right away, regardless of who caused the crash.
intake.legal_medPayHelper: Medical Payments (MedPay) is optional auto coverage that pays your medical bills after a crash regardless of fault. "Not sure" is fine.
intake.legal_whatMedPay: MedPay covers medical bills for you and your passengers after a crash, no matter who was at fault, on top of any health insurance.
intake.legal_plaintiffCarrierHelper: The carrier on your own auto policy, if you have one. Leave blank if you're not sure.
intake.legal_uploadInstead: Have your insurance card or police report? You can upload it instead of typing these details — we'll pull the carrier, policy number, and adjuster from it.
intake.legal_reportedQuestion: Have you reported the accident to insurance?
intake.legal_attorneyQuestion: Do you currently have an attorney?
intake.legal_acceptedQuestion: Have you accepted a settlement offer?
intake.legal_settledWarning: Settled cases may not qualify for attorney review.
intake.review_helper: Quick check before we create the report.
intake.review_confidenceHelper: Based on treatment detail, documents, fault context, and missing information.
intake.review_ready: Ready to generate your ClearCaseIQ assessment. Missing details will be shown as next steps in your report.
intake.consent_approxDate: Based on an approximate incident date. An attorney will confirm the exact deadline.
intake.consent_confirmHelper: Please confirm these permissions so we can display your case analysis.
intake.consent_agreeTerms: I agree to the Terms & Privacy Policy
intake.consent_agreeAi: I consent to AI-assisted case analysis
intake.consent_privacySecure: Your information remains private and secure.

intake.readiness_desc: Your case has strong indicators that attorneys look for.

intake.generatingSubtitle: This usually takes a few seconds — please keep this window open.
intake.draft_savedProgress: We saved your progress, so you can pick up where you left off.
intake.reset_confirmBody: This will clear your answers and restart the intake from the beginning. This can't be undone.
intake.savedAnswer_hint: Tap another option to update your answer.
intake.sol_noDeadline: We could not confirm the deadline from the current facts.
intake.sol_selectState: Select the state and county to check your filing deadline early.
intake.sol_dayRemaining: About {days} day remaining based on your current answers.
intake.sol_daysRemaining: About {days} days remaining based on your current answers.
intake.sol_approxNote: This estimate uses an approximate incident date.
intake.sol_olderNeedsExactDate: Enter your exact incident date to calculate your filing deadline.
intake.sol_olderPrefix: An incident more than 6 months ago may be close to its filing deadline.
intake.sol_basedOnAnswers: Based on your current answers and state laws.
intake.stepTitles_financial_impact: How has this injury affected you financially?
intake.dv_subtitle: Here's what your case may be worth based on the details you've provided.
intake.dv_subtitleEmpty: Tell us about your costs and lost income to estimate your case value.
intake.dv_rangeNote: This is the range most similar cases have settled for out of court.
intake.dv_confSub: Based on the information you've provided.
intake.dv_wellDocumented: Your case is already well documented.
intake.dv_incOngoing: Ongoing treatment — shows injuries still affecting you
intake.dv_incDiagnosis: Diagnosis documented — supports severity of injury
intake.dv_incPT: Physical therapy documented — consistent, appropriate treatment
intake.dv_redPolice: Police report missing — limits liability confirmation
intake.dv_redBills: Medical bills missing — harder to prove economic damages
intake.dv_redShared: Shared liability possible — could reduce settlement amount
intake.dv_redWitness: Limited witness evidence — fewer statements to support your case
intake.dv_redImaging: No imaging on record — weaker proof of injury severity
intake.dv_redWage: No lost-income recorded — missing a damages category
intake.dv_addBills: Upload medical bills — proves economic damages
intake.dv_addWage: Upload wage-loss docs — adds lost-income damages
intake.dv_addMRI: Upload MRI report — supports injury severity
intake.dv_settleNote: Most personal injury cases settle before trial. Trial cases can take 18–36 months to resolve.
intake.dv_compNote: Representative range for this injury type. Your documented case may differ.
intake.dv_howCalcBody: We add your economic damages (past and future medical bills, lost wages, and property damage), then apply a pain-and-suffering multiplier based on injury severity and treatment intensity. Low and high estimates reflect the range similar cases settle within. These figures are estimates, not legal advice, and a final value depends on documentation and the facts of your case.
intake.dv_emptyHint: Add your medical bills, future treatment, and lost income below to see your estimated case value.
intake.error_futureDate: The incident date can't be in the future
intake.error_invalidDate: Enter a valid date (year 1900 or later)
intake.error_submitFailed: Failed to submit. Please try again.
intake.legal_insurerContactQuestion: Has the insurance company contacted you or made an offer?
intake.legal_offerAmountQuestion: About how much was the offer?
intake.legal_coverageLimitsQuestion: Do you know the other party's insurance limits?
intake.legal_coverageLimitsHelper: This helps us estimate the maximum available coverage.
intake.legal_whyLimits: Insurance limits can affect the maximum compensation available in your case.
intake.legal_whatUmUim: This coverage can help pay for your injuries if the at-fault driver has insufficient or no insurance.
intake.legal_policyNote: If you have any policy numbers or adjuster contact information, add them in your case summary.
intake.legal_secureNote: This information is secure and used only to evaluate your case.

results.title: ClearCaseIQ Case Assessment
results.preliminary: Preliminary Estimate Based on Your Answers
results.sendForReview: See if an attorney wants your case
results.uploadHint: Upload medical bills, records, or police reports to increase confidence.

snap.litDesc: Typical case costs, separate from the attorney fee. Pre-suit items usually apply even if you settle; deposition and expert costs apply only if your case is actively litigated. These are normally advanced by your attorney and repaid from the settlement.
snap.litDisclaimer: Estimates only. Court filing (~$400) and service (~$125) fees are fairly standard; medical record retrieval, depositions, and expert witnesses vary widely by case and venue.
snap.topTitle: Top Actions to Strengthen Your Case
snap.topDesc: Complete these steps to improve your evaluation and attract more attorney interest.
snap.actPoliceDesc: Liability is easier to prove with official documentation.
snap.actRecordsDesc: Shows the extent of your injuries and treatment.
snap.actBillsDesc: Documents the economic value of your treatment.
snap.actWageTitle: Add wage verification or pay stubs
snap.actWageDesc: Helps prove lost income and work impact.

submitted.title: Your matter was sent for attorney review
submitted.deliveredSecurely: Your summary was delivered securely to counsel who handle similar cases.
submitted.initialResponses: Initial responses are often received within one business day.
submitted.next3: You choose whether to speak with them
submitted.strengthenTitle: Strengthen your file while you wait
submitted.strengthenDesc: Additional documentation can improve how your matter is assessed.
submitted.saveTitle: Save your case and track attorney responses
submitted.saveDesc: Create a free account to track your case and upload more evidence.
submitted.limitationsBody: This analysis is informational only and not legal advice. Consult qualified counsel before making decisions.

report.fullReportTitle: Full report, sharing, and legal notes
report.fullReportDesc: Open this if you want the complete supplemental analysis.
report.execBullet1: The incident narrative suggests facts that may support liability against another party.
report.execBullet2: Injury and treatment information may support a damages claim.
report.execBullet3: Medical documentation, where present, strengthens the file.
report.attorneyReviewP1: If you choose to proceed, we prepare your matter summary for review and facilitate matching with counsel who handle similar cases.
report.attorneyReviewP2: Initial responses are often received within one business day.
report.attorneyReviewP3: No obligation to retain any particular attorney.
report.panelExperience: Substantial experience in personal injury matters
report.namesRevealed: Attorney names are revealed after you continue to attorney review.
report.filingRemainingSuffix: remaining to file a claim, depending on court rules and tolling.
report.filingWarning: Missing a limitations period can bar recovery. Confirm deadlines with counsel in your jurisdiction.
report.exportDesc: Download a PDF suitable for your records or forward to counsel. The share link is read-only for recipients.
report.limit1: This report is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal advice or a lawyer-client relationship.
report.limit2: Estimates and benchmarks are derived from models and comparable data; actual outcomes depend on facts, law, forum, and parties involved.
report.limit3: Consult a qualified attorney licensed in your jurisdiction before relying on this analysis for any decision.
report.limit4: Past results do not guarantee future outcomes.

headings.snapshotSub: Here's how your case looks based on the information you've provided.

overview.intro: Your case summary, settlement estimate, and attorney matches are at the top of this page. Below is a deeper look at the records and gaps behind those numbers.
overview.noTimeline: No treatment timeline yet. Add medical records to build your timeline.
overview.docsInPlace: Your key documents are in place.
overview.takeSteps: Take these steps to strengthen your case and improve your outcome.
overview.note1: This is an estimate, not a guarantee of outcome.
overview.note2: Case value depends on many factors and may change.
overview.note3: Information is encrypted and only shared with attorneys you choose.
overview.note4: No obligation to hire. You remain in control.

liability.title: Liability Analysis
liability.subtitle: An evaluation of fault, evidence, and factors that impact liability in your case.
liability.aiRearEnd: you reported being rear-ended while stopped at a traffic signal. Rear-end collisions generally create a strong presumption that the trailing driver is at fault.
liability.aiGenericB: was reported with the facts you provided.
liability.aiCausationYes: Your medical treatment shortly after the incident, which supports causation.
liability.aiCausationAdd: Adding medical treatment records would support causation.
liability.aiDispute: However, without a police report, witness statements, or scene photos, the insurance company may dispute fault. Adding those items would significantly strengthen your case.
liability.aiStrong: Your supporting evidence puts you in a strong position on liability.
liability.moreEvidenceNeeded: More evidence needed to increase confidence.
liability.insurerArgue: What an Insurance Company Might Argue
liability.howImproveSub: Estimated liability strength as you add key evidence.
liability.venueHistPrefix: Historical data shows outcomes in this venue tend to be
liability.recommendedStepsSub: These actions will strengthen your liability position the most.
liability.updatedFooter: Liability analysis is updated as new information is added.

medical.title: Medical Story
medical.subtitle: Review your treatment timeline, records, and injuries before attorney handoff.
medical.confirmedDesc: Attorneys will see your treatment timeline as accurate. You can still upload more records anytime to strengthen your case.
medical.reviewTimelineDesc: Check the timeline below and confirm it's accurate before attorneys see your case. Add any missing visits or more records to strengthen it.
medical.startAddingTitle: Start by adding your medical records
medical.startAddingDesc: Attorneys need your treatment records and bills to review your case. No documents yet? Enter quick estimates instead — you can replace them later.
medical.treatmentStrengthSub: Based on treatment length and consistency
medical.attorneyReadinessSub: Upload missing items to improve attorney interest
medical.fromFirstToRecent: From first treatment to most recent visit
medical.aiMedExtra: Additional medical records and bills will help verify your treatment and increase the accuracy of your case value and attorney interest.
medical.tipBody: Upload your medical records and bills to strengthen your case value and improve your chances of attorney representation.
medical.yourTimelineSub: A chronological view of your treatment journey.
medical.noEvents: No treatment events yet. Upload medical records to build your timeline.
medical.allKeyOnFile: All key evidence is on file.
medical.estimatesUpdate: Estimates will update as you add more records and bills.
medical.futureRiskSub: Based on current symptoms and treatment plan
medical.nextBestActionDesc: Upload your medical records and bills to increase your case value estimate and improve your chances of attorney representation.
medical.haveQuestionsDesc: Our team is here to help you understand your medical story and records.
medical.privacyFooter: Your information is private and secure. We only share your case with attorneys if you choose to continue.
medical.reviewCorrect: Review & correct your medical details and estimates
medical.lostWagesNote: This is not included in the medical bill total. Please review wage-loss documents separately because summaries can include duplicate subtotal and total amounts.
medical.noDocsYetDesc: Enter your best estimates for now. Uploaded bills and records can replace these numbers later.
medical.notesPlaceholder: Example: ER bill not received yet; missed about two weeks of work.

documents.subtitle: The right documents can increase your settlement value, improve confidence, and get attorney reviews faster.
documents.biggestImpact: These will have the biggest impact on your case.
documents.criticalDesc: Critical for attorney review and demand package.
documents.strengthensDesc: Strengthens your file and supports your claim.
documents.noGaps: No major missing-document gaps were detected. You look close to attorney-review ready.
documents.yourProgressSub: See how your uploads impact your case.
documents.attorneyReviewReadinessSub: Upload key docs to get faster reviews
documents.demandReadinessSub: Complete to generate a demand package
documents.seeImpactSub: This is how your case could improve with key documents.
documents.docExtractionSub: We read medical records and bills for dates, providers, treatment events, and amounts.
documents.billTotalNote: This is the sum of dollar amounts found in uploaded files marked as bills. Please review for duplicates, summaries, or non-medical amounts.
documents.billTotalEmpty: Upload itemized medical bills so we can total the charges for your case value estimate.
documents.wageLossNote2: We keep wage-loss documents out of the medical bill total. Review this amount separately before relying on it.
documents.demandStatusSub: Generate a professional demand package once you have the required documents.
documents.needHelpDocsSub: Our team can guide you on how to obtain missing records.

value.compositeScore: Composite score from viability and file signals.
value.seeIfAttorney: See if an attorney wants your case
value.settlementDisclaimer: Settlement estimates reflect injury-supported value after settlement compression, liability risk, evidence confidence, venue signals, and insurance constraints. Not a guarantee of outcome.
value.preliminaryTitle: This is a preliminary estimate — and it can grow
value.preliminaryBody: We calculated this range from what you've shared so far. A few key figures are still missing, so treat this number as a conservative floor — not a ceiling. Adding the details below typically increases and sharpens your estimate.
value.preliminaryHint: Don't have exact numbers yet? That's normal this soon after an accident. A rough range is fine — you can refine it later as bills and records come in.
value.litigationPotentialBody: Current information suggests the case may have additional value if medical treatment continues, imaging confirms injury findings, liability evidence strengthens, or wage loss and future care are documented.
value.litigationPotentialNote: Trial outcomes are uncertain, take longer, cost more, and may be limited by collectability or policy limits.
value.baseCaseTypeBody: We start with the case category before adding facts like fault, treatment, documents, venue, insurance, and injury severity. Different claim types begin in different baseline ranges because attorneys and insurers evaluate them with different proof burdens, expected treatment patterns, and liability issues.
value.startingPointA: This is the starting point only. The displayed settlement range of
value.startingPointB: reflects later adjustments from your specific facts and available documents.
value.howBaselineBody: The model does not treat each fact as a fixed dollar add-on. Instead, it treats major facts as modifiers that can increase confidence, reduce risk discounts, move the case into a higher injury band, or keep the estimate conservative.
value.settlementCalcFormula: Injury-supported value x settlement compression x liability risk x evidence confidence x venue / insurance constraints.
value.settlementCalcNote: This is why the explanation says clear liability reduces discounting instead of saying it added a specific dollar amount.
value.trialCalcFormula: Economic damages + non-economic damages + future damages, adjusted by liability, venue, jury risk, and evidence strength.
value.calcExample: Example: clear liability usually improves confidence and reduces the fault discount. It is not modeled as a universal "+$10,000" rule because the dollar impact depends on the size of the case, injury severity, available insurance, and evidence.
value.prepareOwnBody: If you want to try resolving the claim yourself, generate a pro-se settlement demand letter, checklist, and downloadable DOCX. This does not replace legal advice.
value.noDiyFlags: Your current file does not show the common DIY risk flags we screen for.
value.notEnoughBenchmark: We do not have enough benchmark data for this venue yet, so your estimate is based mainly on your facts, injuries, and uploaded records.
value.couldNotCalcDeadline: We could not calculate a filing deadline from the current facts.
value.addIncidentDate: Add the incident date and venue details to confirm this risk.
value.highImpactDesc: High impact on value and attorney review speed.
value.noGapsFromFile: No major missing-document gaps were detected from the current file. You look close to attorney-review ready.

chrome.sharedReadOnly: You're viewing a shared, read-only copy of this case report. Editing is disabled.
chrome.followSteps: Follow these steps to get your case in front of attorneys.
chrome.afterFeesA: After fees & costs, you keep about
chrome.addRecordsSharpen: — add medical records & bills to sharpen this
chrome.rangeBasedOnA: This range is based on the information available so far. Add
chrome.ifTrial: If your case goes to trial
chrome.trialGrossMid: gross figure before attorney fees and costs
chrome.trialGrossB: , and an uncommon outcome — most cases settle. Trials add time, risk, and expense.
chrome.liabilityDefault: Based on the facts provided, fault may rest with the other party. More evidence will strengthen liability.
chrome.attractiveHigh: Your case looks attractive to attorneys based on the current facts.
chrome.potentialLow: Your case has potential, but additional records will help attract more attorney interest.
chrome.whatYouTakeA: What you might actually take home from a
chrome.whatYouTakeB: settlement, after the deductions that typically come out of a personal-injury recovery.
chrome.exhaustedNote: At this settlement estimate, projected attorney fees, medical liens, and case costs would use up most or all of the recovery, leaving little to nothing. You would not owe money out of pocket — and liens and costs are commonly negotiated down to preserve a recovery for you. Your attorney will confirm the final numbers.
chrome.illustrativeNote: Illustrative only. Attorney fees, medical liens, and case costs vary by case and are often negotiated down — your attorney will confirm the final numbers.
chrome.plainLanguage: A plain-language read on your case and the factors that drive its value.
chrome.addWageLoss: Add wage-loss proof to capture this.
chrome.attorneysHandle: Attorneys who regularly handle cases like yours.
chrome.comprehensiveAnalysis: Comprehensive analysis of your case based on the information and documents provided.
